title: Rhyan Console Table
summary: A 1x12 storage console with two euro-slide drawers and acrylic-and-mirror-clip doors below — the console-length companion to this catalog's Rhyan end table.
description:
This console pairs with this catalog's Rhyan end table, applying the same plywood-and-acrylic-door language at console length: a storage box built from 1x12 shelves and dividers, two drawers up top, and a pair of glazed doors below hiding a lower shelf. Drill pocket holes along all sides of the shelves — not just the ends — before assembly, since the front and back edges need holes too even though only the end holes typically show in a diagram.

One of the three shelves gets set aside early and becomes the bottom shelf later; the other two get dividers and sides attached to form the storage box, with pocket holes hidden to the insides wherever possible. **Pre-drill the divider pocket holes on all four sides — front, back, top, and bottom** — while the divider is still a separate, accessible piece, since it's the one part of this build that needs holes in more places than a typical panel.

The face frames mix two pocket-hole settings depending on stock thickness: 3/4" holes for thin material, 1-1/2" holes where 1-1/2"-thick stock joins itself. Keep the frame's actual measurements matched to your real stock as you build, not just the plan numbers. Drawers build the same way as the end table's — sized 1" less than the opening width, with an even 3/4" inset for the drawer face — and the doors close out the piece with acrylic panels on mirror clips, the same glazed-door detail that ties this whole two-piece collection together.

tools.note: If ripping the plywood drawer bottom to size.

materials.name: Acrylic glass and mirror clips, 12 in x 12 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Build to fit your door opening.

steps.title: Drill and trim the shelves
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along all sides of each shelf — front and back as well as the ends. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue the end trim, keeping top edges flush. Set one shelf aside for later as the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Build the storage box
steps.body: From the remaining two shelves, attach the dividers and sides, hiding pocket holes to the insides. Pre-drill the divider's pocket holes on all four sides — front, back, top, and bottom.

steps.title: Build the face frames
steps.body: Build the face frames using 3/4" pocket holes for thinner stock joints and 1-1/2" pocket holes for 1-1/2"-to-1-1/2" stock joints, keeping the frame matched to your actual stock thickness as you build.

steps.title: Attach the face frames
steps.body: Attach the face frames to the box and bottom shelf through the predrilled holes with glue and 3/4" pocket hole screws, adding additional fasteners where needed.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build the drawer boxes 1" less in width than the opening from 1x4s, attach the plywood bottom, and attach the drawer slides. Install so drawers sit inset 3/4" from the outside of the cabinet.

steps.title: Attach drawer faces
steps.body: With the drawers fully pushed in, align the drawer faces with an even 1/8" gap on all sides and nail on with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, avoiding areas where hardware will be added.

steps.title: Build the doors and finish
steps.body: Build the doors from 1x2 stock, test-fit with hinges, then add acrylic glass to the back with mirror clips. Install magnetic clasps. Add knobs and handles. Sand and finish.
materials: Acrylic glass and mirror clips, 12 in x 12 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Console table with two drawers and mirrored acrylic doors
